title: Windows High-Entropy DNS Queries
description: Surfaces DNS queries with unusually long or high-entropy domain names, which are characteristic of DNS tunneling, DGA malware, or data exfiltration over DNS.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Query Name' != null | eval domain_length = length('Query Name') | stats count as query_count, avg(domain_length) as avg_length, max(domain_length) as max_length by 'Host Name' | where avg_length > 40 or max_length > 60 | sort -avg_length
notes: Normal domains average 15-25 characters. Domains over 40 chars often encode data. Look for base64-like subdomains and queries to uncommon TLDs.
